title: Narcissus linnaeanus
taxonomy: Narcissus linnaeanus is classified within the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Amaryllidaceae, and the genus Narcissus. It belongs to the order Asparagales and the subclass Liliidae. Additionally, it is classified as a monocotyledonous plant, indicating that it has one cotyledon or seed leaf. Narcissus linnaeanus also falls under the class Magnoliopsida, which is a group of flowering plants. This classification further categorizes the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships.
uses: Narcissus linnaeanus, also known as the Hoop-petticoat Daffodil, has been used in traditional medicine due to its potential health benefits. The plant contains compounds that may have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various ailments. In traditional medicine, Narcissus linnaeanus is believed to have been used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Its bulbs are also thought to have been used to treat digestive problems and as a diuretic. However, more research is needed to confirm the plant's efficacy and safety for medicinal use.
benefits: Narcissus linnaeanus, also known as the Pyrenean narcissus, is a species of flowering plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and prevent infections. properties: Narcissus linnaeanus, also known as the Pyrenean narcissus, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its therapeutic properties. It is believed to have antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which can be beneficial in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant's extracts have also been shown to have antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Additionally, Narcissus linnaeanus is thought to have a potential role in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of this plant.
constituents: Narcissus linnaeanus, also known as Liliago, is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties.  It contains active constituents such as galantamine, which has been found to have acetylcholinesterase inhibitory properties, potentially useful in the treatment of cognitive disorders. Other constituents of Narcissus linnaeanus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which may have various biological activities. The plant's active constituents are responsible for its traditional uses in folk medicine, where it has been employed to treat a range of ailments. Further research is needed to fully understand the pharmacological effects of these constituents and their potential applications in modern medicine.
parts: Narcissus linnaeanus, also known as the Linnæus' daffodil or Poet's narcissus, is a species of flowering plant. The medicinal parts of Narcissus linnaeanus are primarily the bulbs and the flowers, which contain compounds with potential health benefits. The bulbs are rich in alkaloids, glycosides, and other bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. However, further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential uses of Narcissus linnaeanus.

intro: Narcissus pseudonarcissus, commonly known as Narcissus linnaeanus, is a perennial plant belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Narcissus linnaeanus include alkaloids like galantamine and lycorine, which have been shown to exhibit neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects. In herbal medicine, Narcissus linnaeanus is often prepared as an infusion, tincture, or ointment, and is commonly used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and skin irritations.
